title: Update rules
content:
Use this endpoint to update the details of rules. ## Comparing v1.2 and v1.3 ```xtable |Changes{30%}|v1.2{35%}|v1.3{35%}| |---|---|---| |Endpoint path| /v1.2/optimizer/rule/update/| /v1.3/optimizer/rule/update/| |Request parameter data type |`advertiser_id`: number
`dimensions_id`: number[]
`rule_id`: number |`advertiser_id`: string
`dimensions_id`: string[]
`rule_id`: string | |Response parameter data type|`rule_ids`: number[]|`rule_ids`: string[]| ``` ## Request **Endpoint** **Method** POST **Header** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type
Allowed format: `"application/json"`. | ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |advertiser_id {Required}|string| Advertiser ID | |rules|object[]| List of rules| #|apply_objects {required}|object[]| Objects that you want to apply the rules to. Objects can be campaigns, ad groups, or ads.| ##|dimension {required}|string| Category of the object. Enum values: `CAMPAIGN`, `ADGROUP`, `AD`.| ##|dimension_ids| string[]| IDs of the objects| ##|pre_condition_type {required}|string| Conditions to filter objects that you want to include. Enum values:
  • `SELECTED`: Only include those that are selected by IDs.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_CAMPAIGN`: Select all active campaigns.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD_GROUP`: Select all active ad groups.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD`: Select all active ads.
  • ` ALL_ACTIVE_AD_GROUP_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all active ad groups under the selected campaign.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all active ads under selected dimensions
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_CAMPAIGN`: Select all inactive campaigns.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_GROUP`: Select all inactive ad groups
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD`: Select all inactive ads.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_GROUP_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all inactive ad groups under the selected campaign.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all inactive ads under the selected campaign or ad group (specified by `dimension` and `dimension_ids`)
| #|conditions {required}|object[]| Conditions| ##|subject_type {required}|string| Subjects that the condition will be based on. Enum values:
  • `COST`
  • `DAILY_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE`
  • `LIFETIME_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE`
  • `IMPRESSION`
  • `CLICK`
  • `CONVERSION`
  • `CONVERSION_SKAN`
  • `CVR`
  • `CVR_SKAN`
  • `CPA`
  • `CPM`
  • `CPC`
  • `CTR`
  • `COST_PER_RESULT`
  • `RESULT`
  • `RESULT_RATE`
  • `BID_STRATEGY`: `values` must be one of these: `COST_CAP`, `BID_CAP`, `MAX_CONVERSION`, `LOWEST_COST`.
  • `OPTIMIZATION_GOAL`: `values`must be one of these: `CONVERT`, `CLICK`, `SHOW`, `REACH`, `INSTALL`, `IN_APP_EVENT`, `LEAD_GENERATION`, `TWO_SECOND_VIDEO_VIEW`, `SIX_SECOND_VIDEO_VIEW`
  • `OBJECTIVE`: `values`must be one of these: `TRAFFIC`, `APP_PROMOTION`, `WEB_CONVERSIONS`, `VIDEO_VIEWS`, `REACH`, `LEAD_GENERATION`
  • `NAME`
  • `DAYS_SINCE_CREATION`: `match_type` should be one of `GT`, `LT`, `BETWEEN`, `MATCH`.
  • `NO_CONDITION`: Be careful when setting a rule without any conditions to avoid any unintended changes.
  • `ROAS_PURCHASE`
  • `ROAS_IMPRESSION_ADS`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASE_SKAN`
  • `ROAS_COMPLETE_PAYMENT`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASES_ONSITE`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CPA`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CONVERSION`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CVR`
| ##|range_type|string| Time range for the condition. Enum values:
  • `TODAY`
  • `YESTERDAY`
  • `PAST_THREE_DAYS`
  • `PAST_FIVE_DAYS`
  • `PAST_SEVEN_DAYS`
  • `LIFETIME`
| ##|match_type|string| Operator to connect a condition with a value. Enum values: `GT` (greater than), `LT` (less than), `BETWEEN`, `MATCH`. Only when subject_type is `NAME`, can below match_type be used:
  • `CONTAINS`
  • `NOT_CONTAINS`
  • `START_WITH`
  • `END_WITH`
  • `STRING_EQUAL` | ##|values|string[]| Values for the condition. If `match_type` is `BETWEEN`, include two values in this array

    Note: Verification rules for the `values` field vary with the `subject_type` you pass in. See [Verification Rules for values](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?rid=7xusz43oh3h&id=1738767670852610#item-link-Verification Rules for values) for details.| ##|calculation_type|string|The `subject_type` has to be `COST`. Set to `OF_EACH_OBJECT` by default if this field is not provided.

  • `ALL_OBJECTS`
  • `OF_EACH_OBJECT`
    • | #|actions {required}|object[]| Actions to be taken when the condition is met| ##|subject_type {required}|string| Type of actions to take.

    Enum values: `TURN_ON`, `TURN_OFF`, `MESSAGE`, `DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFERIME_BUDGET`, `BID`| ##|action_type|string| Enum values: `INCREASE`, `DECREASE`, `ADJUST_TO`| ##|value_type|string| Enum values: `EXACT`, `PERCENT`| ##|value|object|
    • When `action_type` is `INCREASE` or `DECREASE`, users can specify the maximum (if `action_type` is `INCREASE`) or minimum (if `action_type` is `DECREASE`) budget using `use_limit` and `limit`.
    • When `action_type` is `ADJUST_TO`, you don't need to provide `use_limit` and `limit`.

    For example, if `action_type` is `INCREASE`, and `value_type` is `PERCENT`, `value` object is `{"value": 20.0, "use_limit":true, "limit": 100.0}`, then when the condition is met, the budget will be increased by 20.0% but the budget will not exceed $100.0. | ###| value | float | Valid when `action_type` is `ADJUST_TO`.

    The exact value to be adjusted to. | ###| limit | float | Valid when `action_type` is `INCREASE` or `DECREASE`.

    • If `action_type` is `INCREASE`, this field means the maximum number limit.
    • If `action_type` is `DECREASE`, this field means the minimum number limit.

    If you configure this field, you need to set `use_limit` to `true` at the same time. | ###| use_limit | boolean | Valid when `action_type` is `INCREASE` or `DECREASE`.

    Whether to set a maximum or minimum number limit (`limit`). | ##|frequency_info|object| Frequency settings. It can be used when the action `subject_type` is `DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFERIME_BUDGET`, or `BID`| ###|type|string| Enum values:
    • `ONLY_ONCE`
    • `ONCE_IN_24_H`
    • `ONCE_IN_48_H`
    • `ONCE_IN_1_W`
    • `CUSTOM`
    | ###|custom_frequency_type|string|Custom frequency type. Valid only when `type` is `CUSTOM`. Enum values:
    • `N_MINTUE_Y_TIMES`
    • `N_HOUR_Y_TIMES`
    • `N_DAY_Y_TIMES`
    | ###|time|number|It is the `N` in `custom_frequency_type`| ###|count|number|It is the `Y` in `custom_frequency_type`| #|notification {required}|object| Notification settings| ##|notification_type {required}|string|Notification type you need. Enum values:
    • `TASK_FINISH`: Notification will be sent when the task is finished. This option is only for `MESSAGE` subject type.
    • `ANY_CHANGES`: If the `subject_type` for the action is not `MESSAGE` but you still want to receive emails, use this option.
    • `NOT_NOTIFICATION`: No notifications are needed.
    | ##|email_setting|object|Email notification settings| ###|notification_period|string|Enum values:
    • `EVERY_TIME`
    • `TIME_SCHEDULE`: notifications will be sent together around a specified time.
    | ###|email_exec_time|string[]|Required when `notification_period` is `TIME_SCHEDULE`. Notification email sent time. For example, "12:00".| ###|no_result_notification|boolean|It will be set as false by default if not provided, meaning no notification will be sent if there is no result.| ###|mute_option|string|This field can be used to mute or unmute email notification for the day. Enum values:
    • `MUTE`
    • `UNMUTE`
    | #|rule_exec_info {required}|object| Rule run settings| ##|exec_time_type {required}|string|When the rule will be run. Enum values:
    • `PER_HALF_HOUR`: the rule will be run every half hour.
    • `CUSTOM`: the rule will be run at a customized time (specified in the `exec_time` field).
    • `HALF_HOUR_IN_SPECIFIC_TIME_PERIOD`: the rule will be run every 30 minutes during specified time range on specifed days during the week.
    • `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`: the rule will be run only once at a customized time (specified in the `exec_time` field).
    | ##|exec_time|string| If `exec_time_type` is set to `CUSTOM` or `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`, use this field to specify the exact time when the rule will be run.
    Format:
    • "HH:MM" when `exec_time_type` is `CUSTOM`. For example: "12:00".
    • "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM" when `exec_time_type` is `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`. For example: "2023-04-20 04:20".
    Note: If `exec_time_type` = `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`, the `exec_time` cannot exceed one year from the current time.| ##|time_period_info|object[]|Valid when `exec_time_type` is `HALF_HOUR_IN_SPECIFIC_TIME_PERIOD`. It has `start_time`, `end_time`, `date_type` and `num` to specify the time period information.| ###|num{+Conditional}|integers[]|Required when `date_type` is `WEEK` or `MONTH`. Each number in the list represents the day in the week or month.
    • When `date_type` is `WEEK`, value range for this field is [1, 7], meaning Monday to Sunday.
    • When `date_type` is `MONTH`, value range for this field is [1, 28], meaning 1st day to the 28th day of the month.
    | ###|start_time|string| Start time of this time period. For example, "12:00".| ###|end_time|string| End time of this time period. For example, "12:00".| ###|date_type|string| Type of date. Enum values: `DAY`, `WEEK`, `MONTH`.| #|tzone|string|User timezone. In create/update rules, it's used with `mute_option` in notification.email_setting. For acceptable timezone code, see [Time Zone](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737586324313089).| #|rule_id {required}|string| ID of the rule| #|name {required}|string| Name of the rule| |lang|string| Language of the error messages. Enum values: `ZH` (Simplified Chinese), `EN` (English)(default), `JA` (Japanese).| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code JSON JSON) { "advertiser_id":75960823018, "rules":[ { "apply_objects":[ { "dimension":"CAMPAIGN", "dimension_ids":[1694011873852465], "pre_condition_type":"SELECTED" } ], "conditions":[ { "subject_type":"CLICK", "range_type":"LIFETIME", "match_type":"GT", "values":["1"] } ], "actions":[{ "subject_type":"MESSAGE" }], "notification":{ "notification_type":"TASK_FINISH" }, "rule_exec_info":{ "exec_time_type":"PER_HALF_HOUR", "exec_time":"" }, "name":"[API]Automated Rule--example-update", "rule_id":6984697088064290818 } ] } (/code) ``` ## Response ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |request_id|string| Unique ID of the request| |data|object| Returned data| #|rule_ids |string[]| IDs of the rules that have been created or updated| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code JSON JSON) { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "202107290645040102450631993207A638", "data": { "rule_ids": [ 6984697088064290818 ] } } (/code) ```
